title: Racosperma leptaleum

intro: Racosperma leptaleum, also known as Rhus leptaleum, is a plant species that belongs to the family Anacardiaceae, commonly referred to as the cashew or poison ivy family. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian medicine, and its extracts have been reported to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
